bio: Berend "Ben" Lourens was born in Bennekom, Ede, Gelderland, on 13 January 1911, with his father Albertus recorded as a farmer. His Iowa obituary says he came to America at age two with his family, married Gertrude Van Dusseldorp in Monroe on 25 February 1937, and farmed for years near Prairie City and Runnells before moving into Prairie City in 1984. He died in 2003 at age 92 and was buried after services at First Reformed Church in Prairie City.
funFacts: In retirement Ben enjoyed woodworking and gardening.
His obituary names two surviving sons, five grandchildren, seven great-grandchildren, and a large Lourens sibling group.
immigration: Ben's obituary reports that he came to America at age two with his family; since his Dutch birth record is dated 13 January 1911, the family immigration was about 1913.
records: 1911 civil birth registration, Bennekom/Ede, Gelderland (Berend Lourens)
2003 IAGenWeb obituary, Jasper County, Iowa

bio: Albertus Lourens was recorded as a 26-year-old farmer from Ede when he married Aartje Snetselaar in Ede on 3 March 1900. He was again recorded as a farmer in the 1911 Bennekom birth record of his son Berend (Ben) Lourens. Ben's obituary says Ben came to America at age two with his family, and an obituary for son Albert places the family in rural Prairie City, Iowa, by Albert's birth on 1 December 1913.
funFacts: The obituary of his son Albert names a large sibling group: Garrett, John, Ben, Orie, George, Gertrude, Bertha, Elizabeth, Pete, Geurt, and Jeanett.
Dutch records show the family within Ede municipality before emigration, including Ede, Veenendaal, Bennekom, and Lunteren-area records.
immigration: Ben Lourens's obituary reports that Ben came to America at age two with his family; because Ben was born in January 1911 in Bennekom and Albert was born in rural Prairie City in December 1913, Albertus and Aartje likely immigrated with their children about 1913.
records: 1900 civil marriage registration, Ede, Gelderland
1911 civil birth registration of son Berend Lourens, Bennekom/Ede
2001 IAGenWeb obituary of son Albert Lourens

bio: Aartje Snetselaar was born at Lunteren on 5 February 1879 to Geurt Snetselaar and Jannetje Jacobsen; her father was recorded as a laborer. She married farmer Albertus Lourens at Ede on 3 March 1900. Her son Ben's obituary says he came to America at age two with his family, placing Aartje in the Lourens family's move from Gelderland to Iowa about 1913.
funFacts: Aartje's records connect three generations of the line: her parents Geurt and Jannetje, her husband Albertus, and her son Berend/Ben.
An obituary for son Albert shows the family became part of the Prairie City/Pella-area Dutch community in Iowa.
immigration: Ben Lourens's obituary reports that Ben came to America at age two with his family, so Aartje appears to have immigrated with Albertus and their children about 1913.
records: 1879 civil birth registration, Lunteren/Ede
1900 civil marriage registration, Ede

bio: Peter appears in Dutch civil records as Peter Louwrens/Lourens, born at Veenendaal on 14 April 1833 to Johannes Louwrens and Hendrika Jeurs. At his 21 June 1862 marriage to Gerritje Aartsen in Ede, he was age 29 and recorded as a farmer. He was still listed as farmer and father of the groom when his son Albertus married Aartje Snetselaar in 1900.
funFacts: His surname appears with both Louwrens and Lourens spellings in civil records.
Peter's 1833 birth record names his father Johannes as a day laborer, while Peter himself was recorded as a farmer by marriage age.
records: 1833 civil birth registration, Veenendaal
1862 civil marriage registration, Ede
1900 civil marriage registration of son Albertus, Ede

bio: Gerritje Aartsen was recorded as a 26-year-old bride born in Stichtsch Veenendaal when she married Peter Lourens in Ede on 21 June 1862. The marriage record names her parents as Aart Aartsen and Jacoba Hartman and records no occupation for Gerritje. In 1900 she appears again in the Ede marriage record of her son Albertus Lourens to Aartje Snetselaar.
funFacts: Her 1862 marriage record ties the Lourens line back to the Aartsen/Hartman family of the Veenendaal area.
She lived to see son Albertus marry into the Snetselaar family, the couple who later anchored the Iowa Lourens line.
records: 1862 civil marriage registration, Ede
1900 civil marriage registration of son Albertus, Ede

Open Archives marriage record for Albertus Lourens and Aartje Snetselaar

bio: Johannes appears in the 1833 Veenendaal birth record of son Peter Louwrens as a 30-year-old dagloner, or day laborer. When Peter married Gerritje Aartsen in 1862, Johannes was again named as father of the groom and recorded as an arbeider, or laborer. These records place Johannes in the working-class Veenendaal/Ede community before later Lourens generations became farmers.
funFacts: Two records nearly thirty years apart record Johannes in laboring occupations.
His son's records show the family surname transitioning between Louwrens and Lourens spellings.
records: 1833 civil birth registration of son Peter Louwrens, Veenendaal
1862 civil marriage registration of son Peter Lourens, Ede

citations.label: Open Archives birth record for Peter Louwrens
Open Archives marriage record for Peter Lourens and Gerritje Aartsen

bio: Hendrika Jeurs appears as the mother of Peter Louwrens in his 1833 Veenendaal birth record, where she was recorded as age 32 and with no occupation stated. She was again named as Peter's mother in his 1862 Ede marriage record to Gerritje Aartsen. These two civil records link Hendrika directly to the Veenendaal Lourens/Louwrens household.
funFacts: Her surname is a key maternal surname in the Lourens line, preserved in both Peter's birth and marriage registrations.
The records show the family staying in the Veenendaal/Ede area across at least 1833-1862.
records: 1833 civil birth registration of son Peter Louwrens, Veenendaal
1862 civil marriage registration of son Peter Lourens, Ede

Open Archives marriage record for Peter Lourens and Gerritje Aartsen

bio: Geurt Snetselaar was born at Ede on 1 July 1854 to Breunis Snetselaar and Aartje Mora; his father was recorded as a laborer. Geurt himself was recorded as a laborer when he married Jannetje Jacobsen in Ede on 25 May 1878. By his death in Ede on 19 December 1923, the civil death record recorded him as a farmer, age 69, born in Ede.
funFacts: His 1923 death record names three (ex-)partners: Maaike van der Wielen, Jannetje Jacobsen, and Hermijntje Schimmel.
He is a clear example of occupational mobility in the line, appearing first as laborer and later as farmer.
records: 1854 civil birth registration, Ede
1878 civil marriage registration, Ede
1923 civil death registration, Ede

bio: Jannetje Jacobsen was recorded as a 20-year-old bride born in Barneveld when she married Geurt Snetselaar in Ede on 25 May 1878. The marriage record names her parents as Berend Jacobsen, a laborer, and Elisabeth Boshuis. She appears again as mother in the 1879 Lunteren birth record of daughter Aartje Snetselaar, who later married Albertus Lourens and immigrated to Iowa.
funFacts: Her daughter Aartje connects the Jacobsen/Boshuis family into the later Lourens immigrant family in Iowa.
Geurt's 1923 death record still names Jannetje among his (ex-)partners.
records: 1878 civil marriage registration, Ede
1879 civil birth registration of daughter Aartje Snetselaar, Lunteren/Ede
1923 civil death registration of husband Geurt Snetselaar, Ede

bio: Breunis appears as Breunis Snetselaar/Snitselaar in Ede civil records. He was recorded as a laborer in the 1854 birth record of son Geurt and in Geurt's 1878 marriage record. His 1883 Ede death record gives him as a 61-year-old laborer born in Lunteren, son of Wijnand Snitselaar and Geertrui Hendriks, and partner of Aartje Mora.
funFacts: His surname is recorded in both Snetselaar and Snitselaar forms, while his father appears as Wijnand Snitselaar.
The 1883 death record identifies Breunis's father Wijnand as a farmer, contrasting with Breunis's own laborer occupation.
records: 1854 civil birth registration of son Geurt Snetselaar, Ede
1878 civil marriage registration of son Geurt Snetselaar, Ede
1883 civil death registration, Ede

bio: Aartje Mora married Breunis Snitselaar at Ede on 6 March 1852. The marriage record includes the note "wettiging 1 kind," indicating the legal recognition of one child at the marriage. She appears as the mother of Geurt Snetselaar in his 1854 Ede birth record and in Geurt's 1878 Ede marriage record to Jannetje Jacobsen.
funFacts: The 1852 marriage record's legitimization note is an unusually specific family-event detail.
Her descendants connect the Mora/Snetselaar line to the Lourens immigrant family through granddaughter Aartje Snetselaar.
records: 1852 civil marriage registration, Ede
1854 civil birth registration of son Geurt Snetselaar, Ede
1878 civil marriage registration of son Geurt Snetselaar, Ede

bio: Berend Jacobsen was born at Ede on 17 January 1822 to Jan Jacobsen, a day laborer, and Teunisje Berends. In the 1878 Ede marriage record of daughter Jannetje Jacobsen to Geurt Snetselaar, Berend was recorded as a laborer. His 1893 death record gives him as a 71-year-old day laborer born in Ede who died at Kallenbroek, with Lisebeth Boshuis named as partner.
funFacts: The Jacobsen line shows repeated laboring occupations: Berend's father was a day laborer, and Berend was later recorded as laborer/day laborer himself.
His daughter Jannetje's marriage brought the Jacobsen/Boshuis family into the Snetselaar line.
records: 1822 civil birth registration, Ede
1878 civil marriage registration of daughter Jannetje Jacobsen, Ede
1893 civil death registration, Kallenbroek/Barneveld

citations.label: Open Archives birth record for Berend Jacobsen
Open Archives marriage record for Geurt Snetselaar and Jannetje Jacobsen

bio: Lisabeth/Elisabeth Boshuis appears in the 1878 Ede marriage record of her daughter Jannetje Jacobsen to Geurt Snetselaar; the record gives no occupation for her. She is also named as Lisebeth Boshuis in the 1893 death record of Berend Jacobsen, where she is identified as his partner or former partner. These records place her in the Barneveld/Ede network that led into the Snetselaar and Lourens families.
funFacts: Her name appears with multiple spellings: Elisabeth in the 1878 marriage record and Lisebeth in Berend Jacobsen's 1893 death record.
Through daughter Jannetje and granddaughter Aartje Snetselaar, the Boshuis line connects to the Lourens immigrants who settled in Iowa.
records: 1878 civil marriage registration of daughter Jannetje Jacobsen, Ede
1893 civil death registration of Berend Jacobsen, Kallenbroek/Barneveld
